findings.problem: The chapter gives Nori two incompatible maternal mappings. Line 65 has Bett say "Hala's her mum," but line 197 calls Nori Bett's daughter. That breaks continuity with the canonical Bett/Nori relationship and muddies the Dao family map in a novel whose central pressure depends on readers tracking kinship precisely.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the relationship wording in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:59, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:65, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:67, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:197 so Nori's status is legible in one consistent scheme. If the intent is biological mother versus adoptive/social mother, make that distinction explicit in the line-65 exchange and keep the same logic in the later narration; otherwise remove the Hala-as-mum wording and align both passages to Bett's established role.
findings.acceptance_test: A close read of revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:59, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:65, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:67, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:197 yields one unambiguous answer to who is parenting Nori, with no passage naming Hala as her mother while a later passage names Bett as her daughter unless the biological/adoptive split is stated on the page.

findings.problem: After the cracked-blue-lid lunchbox image lands, the opening keeps re-explaining Jin's abandonment shame across four consecutive paragraphs. The material is emotionally correct, but the chapter starts paraphrasing its own wound instead of trusting the concrete image and moving pressure forward, which keeps the opening below shortlist-level authority.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the abandonment run in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:13, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:17, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:19. Keep the lunchbox, note, and one socially humiliating consequence, but cut the reiterative abstract framing so each surviving paragraph in this block either adds a new fact, sharpens a concrete consequence, or pivots the chapter back toward Melie in the present scene.
findings.acceptance_test: In revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:13, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:17, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:19, no more than one paragraph exists primarily to explain Jin's shame in abstract terms; the other paragraphs each contribute distinct material by showing the departure image, the lie to Melie, or a concrete social consequence before the chapter resumes present-time action.

findings.problem: The prose leans repeatedly on the same explanatory template: 'the practiced stillness of someone who...', 'the look of someone doing...', 'the low, clear register of someone who...', 'the competence of someone who...'. Once or twice this reads as Jin interpreting people. Repeated across the chapter, it becomes a visible tic that pre-interprets behavior instead of letting observation carry the force.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the repeated-template sentences at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:83, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:119, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:139, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:143, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:145, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:199. Keep Jin's ability to recognize competence and strain, but vary the syntax and render several of these beats through direct physical action, sound, or spatial detail rather than generalized 'someone who...' shorthand.
findings.acceptance_test: Across revised snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:83, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:119, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:139, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:143, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:145, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:199, no more than two sentences use the repeated 'the [noun/look/register/competence] of someone who...' frame, and at least three of those moments are conveyed through direct behavior or sensory detail without explanatory occupational shorthand.

summary: Chapter 06 gets the central medical crisis right: Jin's close-third interior stays materially grounded, Bett's dialogue and emergency competence match the style bible, and Melie's anaphylaxis is rendered with the physical specificity the book requires. It still fails overall because a HIGH continuity contradiction scrambles the Dao family relationship map, the opening over-explains Jin's abandonment shame after its best image has already landed, and the prose falls into a repeated 'someone who...' explanatory template often enough to feel mechanical.
